actionable_feedback: Open scan_fault_localization.xlsx in Excel (or install openpyxl) and verify that all intermediate calculations are implemented as formulas (no hard-coded intermediate numeric results).

Explicitly implement the declared alternative priority computation in spreadsheet cells (e.g., a named range or cell labeled 'priority_score = rank_score' and show the formula cell that derives it), and ensure row-level cells reference the source packet fields.

Add a clearly labeled sensitivity perturbation table in the workbook that programmatically performs +/-5% perturbations and flags any classification flips; include a cell 'expert_review_flag' that is TRUE if any flip occurs.
Add short in-sheet comments or a 'notes' worksheet that cross-links the workbook cells/tables to the debug note row-level citations (record IDs and cell addresses).
Include an explicit tie-break statement implemented in the workbook (labeled cell) mirroring the debug note so automated checks can read it.
Re-run verification after the workbook is opened so the verifier can lift the remaining limitation and confirm semantic implementation.
